title: Create simple blog app with Firebase
content:
Today we will learn how to use Firebase to create a simple blog post app.

Firebase

We will use the following Builds to implement a simple blog app.

  • Authentication: for authentication, in this article, we will use Google SignIn method.
  • Cloud Firestore Database: for storing post datas.
  • Storage: Optional, if you want to post with cover images.

Create project

Go to Firebase console, create a new project and complete the instructions of steps.

Build

Authentication

Click the Authentication categorized in Build from the left bar drawer, and click Get Started.

Choose a sign in method for the authentication of your project, we will use Google SignIn method in this article.

Firestore Database

Click the Firestore Database categorized in Build from the left bar drawer, and click Create Database.

小提示

Why don’t we use Realtime Database instead? Realtime Database stores data into a huge JSON tree, while Firestore Database stores data and organzied them as collections in documents. With this feature, you can search and filter data easier than the former one.

There are two modes to activate the database:

  1. Release mode: The data stored is private, and will decide whether write and read data according to the rules had set.
  2. Debug mode: The data stored is public, and allows to be written and read. By default, you need to update the rules in 30 days in case you need to keep the permission of writing and reading data.
allow read, write: if
    request.time < timestamp.date(2023, 2, 27);

To enable the data always be written and read, i.e. ignoring the date limit, set them to true.

allow read, write: true

Thus, you have the following rules.

rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match /{document=**} {
      allow read, write: true
    }
  }
}

Storage

Click the Storage categorized in Build from the left bar drawer, and click Get Started.

Like the Firestore Database, you will to decide which mode it will activate and the rules it will use.

Import

Go back to your Project Overview, and click Android icon to add Firebase to your android project.

There are several steps:

Register Applications

You will need to provide your android project’s package name and SHA-1 debug key.

小提示

To simply get the SHA-1 key, just type this command:

.gradlew signingReport

If it says permission denied, grant a permission for it then type the command above again.

chmod +x gradlew

Download & Import settings files

Download the google-services.json file of your project and put it inside /app/ directory.

Import Firebase SDK

特别注意

If you are using Android Studio Bumblebee, which uses newer structure starting from Gradle 7.x, the offical instructions will not suit in this way.

Add classpath of google services

Normally, you will see this code in the build.gradle of your project’s root directory.

plugins {
    id 'com.android.application' version '7.3.1' apply false
    id 'com.android.library' version '7.3.1' apply false
}

Add the classpath in this file.

buildscript {
    dependencies {
        classpath 'com.google.gms:google-services:4.3.15'
    }
}

Add plugin of google services

Add this plugin in the build.gradle of your project’s app directory.

plugins {
    ...
    id 'com.google.gms.google-services'
}

Add dependencies

implementation 'com.google.android.gms:play-services-auth:20.4.1'

implementation platform('com.google.firebase:firebase-bom:31.2.0')
implementation 'com.google.firebase:firebase-auth'
implementation 'com.google.firebase:firebase-firestore'
implementation 'com.google.firebase:firebase-storage'

Program

FirebaseAuthUtil.java

Basics

Declare a request code for permission

public static int REQ_CODE = 100;

Check if an account is authenticated

public static boolean isAuthenticated(FirebaseAuth auth) {
    return auth.getCurrentUser() != null;
}

Get Google SignIn options

public static GoogleSignInOptions getGoogleSignInOptions() {
    return new GoogleSignInOptions.Builder(GoogleSignInOptions.DEFAULT_SIGN_IN)
            .requestIdToken(App.getContext().getResources().getString(R.string.default_web_client_id))
            .requestEmail()
            .build();
}

Get Google SignIn clients

public static GoogleSignInClient getGoogleSignInClient(Context context, GoogleSignInOptions options) {
    return GoogleSignIn.getClient(context, options);
}

Sign In

Setup Google SignIn

public static void setupGoogleSignIn(Activity activity, GoogleSignInClient client, int requestCode) {
    Intent intent = client.getSignInIntent();
    activity.startActivityForResult(intent, requestCode);
}

Start Google SignIn

public static void startGoogleSignIn(FirebaseAuth auth, int requestCode, @Nullable Intent data, Callable<?> actionSuccess, Callable<?> actionFailure) {
    if (requestCode == REQ_CODE){
        Task<GoogleSignInAccount> task1 = GoogleSignIn.getSignedInAccountFromIntent(data);
        try {
            GoogleSignInAccount account = task1.getResult(ApiException.class);
            AuthCredential authCredential = GoogleAuthProvider.getCredential(account.getIdToken(), null);
            auth.signInWithCredential(authCredential).addOnCompleteListener(task2 -> {
                if (task2.isSuccessful()) {
                    try {
                        actionSuccess.call();
                    } catch (Exception e) {
                        e.printStackTrace();
                    }
                } else {
                    try {
                        actionFailure.call();
                    } catch (Exception e) {
                        e.printStackTrace();
                    }
                }
            });
        } catch (ApiException e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }
}

Sign Out

Start SignOut

public static void startSignOut(FirebaseAuth auth) {
    auth.signOut();
}

Start Google SignOut

startGoogleSignOut() method is to sign out the google account, users will need to select accounts to sign in again, once they have just logout. If you don’t want to select again after logging out, just use startSignOut() method.

public static void startGoogleSignOut(FirebaseAuth auth, GoogleSignInClient client, Callable<?> action) {
    startSignOut(auth);
    client.signOut().addOnCompleteListener(task ->  {
        try {
            action.call();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    });

MainActivity.java

Preparations before-hand

Declare the following variables in MainActivity.

private GoogleSignInOptions signInOptions;
private GoogleSignInClient signInClient;
private FirebaseAuth auth;

Initialize Firebase.

auth = FirebaseAuth.getInstance();
signInOptions = FirebaseAuthUtil.getGoogleSignInOptions();
signInClient = FirebaseAuthUtil.getGoogleSignInClient(this, signInOptions);

Usages

Check if the account is authenticated, if so, navigate to auth signed screen with Intent.

private void gotoAuthSignedScreen() {
    startActivity(new Intent(this, HomeActivity.class));
}
if (FirebaseAuthUtil.isAuthenticated(auth)) {
    gotoAuthSignedScreen();
}

Click the button to do sign in.

binding.btnSignInGoogle.setOnClickListener(v -> setupGoogleSignIn());
@Override
protected void onActivityResult(int requestCode, int resultCode, @Nullable Intent data) {
    super.onActivityResult(requestCode, resultCode, data);
    startGoogleSignIn(data);
}

private void setupGoogleSignIn() {
    if (!FirebaseAuthUtil.isAuthenticated(auth)) {
        FirebaseAuthUtil.setupGoogleSignIn(this, signInClient, FirebaseAuthUtil.REQ_CODE);
    }
}

private void startGoogleSignIn(Intent data) {
    FirebaseAuthUtil.startGoogleSignIn(auth, FirebaseAuthUtil.REQ_CODE, data, () -> {
        // success
        gotoAuthSignedScreen();
        return null;
    }, () -> {
        // failed
        return null;
    });
}

List all available Blogs

private void setupBlogsList() {
    list = new ArrayList<>();
    FirebaseFirestore.getInstance().collection("Blogs").orderBy("timestamp").addSnapshotListener((value, error) -> {
        list.clear();
        for (DocumentSnapshot snapshot:value.getDocuments()){
            model = snapshot.toObject(Model.class);
            model.setId(snapshot.getId());
            list.add(model);
        }
        adapter.notifyDataSetChanged();
    });
    adapter = new CodesListAdapter(list);
    LinearLayoutManager linearLayoutManager = new LinearLayoutManager(this);
    linearLayoutManager.setStackFromEnd(true);
    linearLayoutManager.setReverseLayout(true);
    binding.rvCodesList.setLayoutManager(linearLayoutManager);
    binding.rvCodesList.setAdapter(adapter);
}

PublishActivity.java

Import Dexter dependency:

implementation 'com.karumi:dexter:6.2.3'

publish blog post method

private void publishBlog() {

    Dexter.withContext(this).withPermissions(Manifest.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, Manifest.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE).withListener(new MultiplePermissionsListener() {
        @Override
        public void onPermissionsChecked(MultiplePermissionsReport multiplePermissionsReport) {
            if (multiplePermissionsReport.areAllPermissionsGranted()) {

                String title = binding.etTitle.getText().toString();
                String content = binding.etContent.getText().toString();
                String author = FirebaseAuthUtil.getAccountDisplayName();

                String date = (String) DateFormat.format("dd", new Date());
                String month = (String) DateFormat.format("MM", new Date());
                String finalDate = date + "/" + month;

                HashMap<String, String> map = new HashMap<>();
                map.put("title", title);
                map.put("content", content);
                map.put("author", author);
                map.put("date", finalDate);
                map.put("timestamp", String.valueOf(System.currentTimeMillis()));
                map.put("shareCount", "0");

                FirebaseFirestore.getInstance().collection("Blogs").document().set(map).addOnCompleteListener(task -> {
                    if (task.isSuccessful()) {
                    }
                });

            }
        }

        @Override
        public void onPermissionRationaleShouldBeShown(List<PermissionRequest> list, PermissionToken permissionToken) {
            permissionToken.continuePermissionRequest();
        }

    }).withErrorListener(dexterError -> finish()).onSameThread().check();

}

ReadActivity.java

read a single blog post

private void readBlog() {

    FirebaseFirestore.getInstance().collection("Blogs").document(id).addSnapshotListener((value, error) -> {
        binding.etTitle.setText(value.getString("title"));
        binding.etContent.setText(value.getString("content"));

        String title = value.getString("title");
        String content = value.getString("content");
        String shareCount = value.getString("shareCount");

        int i_count=Integer.parseInt(shareCount);
        int n_count;
        n_count = i_count + 1;
    });

}

Source codes

// Google Play services
implementation 'com.google.android.gms:play-services-auth:20.4.1'

// Firebase
implementation platform('com.google.firebase:firebase-bom:31.2.0')
implementation 'com.google.firebase:firebase-auth'
implementation 'com.google.firebase:firebase-firestore'
implementation 'com.google.firebase:firebase-storage'

// Glide
implementation 'com.github.bumptech.glide:glide:4.14.2'
annotationProcessor 'com.github.bumptech.glide:compiler:4.14.2'

// Dexter
implementation 'com.karumi:dexter:6.2.3'

layout_list_item.xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content"
    xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto">

    <LinearLayout
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:orientation="vertical"
        android:paddingTop="20dp"
        android:paddingStart="20dp"
        android:paddingEnd="20dp">

        <TextView
            android:id="@+id/tvTitle"
            android:layout_width="wrap_content"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:layout_marginBottom="10dp"
            android:text="title"
            android:textSize="16sp"/>

        <TextView
            android:id="@+id/tvAuthor"
            android:layout_width="wrap_content"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:layout_marginBottom="0dp"
            android:maxLines="3"
            android:text="author"
            android:textSize="12sp"
            android:textColor="?colorAccent"/>

        <TextView
            android:id="@+id/tvShareCount"
            android:visibility="gone"
            android:layout_width="wrap_content"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:layout_marginTop="7dp"
            android:layout_marginBottom="0dp"
            android:text="share count"/>

        <TextView
            android:id="@+id/tvDate"
            android:visibility="gone"
            android:layout_width="wrap_content"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:text="date"/>

    </LinearLayout>

</LinearLayout>

public class Model {

    String title, content, author, date, shareCount, id, timestamp;

    public Model() {

    }

    public Model(String title, String content, String author, String date, String shareCount, String id, String timestamp) {
        this.title = title;
        this.content = content;
        this.author = content;
        this.date = date;
        this.shareCount = shareCount;
        this.id = id;
        this.timestamp = timestamp;
    }

    public void setTitle(String title) {
        this.title = title;
    }

    public String getTitle() {
        return title;
    }

    public void setContent(String content) {
        this.content = content;
    }

    public String getContent() {
        return content;
    }

    public void setAuthor(String author) {
        this.author = author;
    }

    public String getAuthor() {
        return author;
    }

    public void setDate(String date) {
        this.date = date;
    }

    public String getDate() {
        return date;
    }

    public void setShareCount(String shareCount) {
        this.shareCount = shareCount;
    }

    public String getShareCount() {
        return shareCount;
    }

    public void setId(String id) {
        this.id = id;
    }

    public String getId() {
        return id;
    }

    public void setTimestamp(String timestamp) {
        this.timestamp = timestamp;
    }

    public String getTimestamp() {
        return timestamp;
    }

}

public class BlogsListAdapter extends RecyclerView.Adapter<BlogsListAdapter.ViewHolder> {

    ArrayList<Model> list;

    public BlogsListAdapter(ArrayList<Model> list) {
        this.list = list;
        this.notifyDataSetChanged();
    }
    public void filter_list(ArrayList<Model> filter_list){
        list = filter_list;
        notifyDataSetChanged();
    }

    @NonNull
    @Override
    public ViewHolder onCreateViewHolder(@NonNull ViewGroup parent, int viewType) {
        View view = LayoutInflater.from(parent.getContext()).inflate(R.layout.layout_list_item, parent, false);
        return new ViewHolder(view);
    }

    @Override
    public void onBindViewHolder(@NonNull ViewHolder holder, int position) {
        Model model = list.get(position);
        holder.title.setText(model.getTitle());
        holder.date.setText(model.getDate());
        holder.share_count.setText(model.getShareCount());
        holder.author.setText(model.getAuthor());

        holder.itemView.setOnClickListener(v -> {
            Intent intent = new Intent(holder.author.getContext(), ReadActivity.class);
            intent.putExtra("id", model.getId());
            holder.author.getContext().startActivity(intent);
        });
    }

    @Override
    public int getItemCount() {
        return list.size();
    }

    public class ViewHolder extends RecyclerView.ViewHolder {
        ImageView img;
        TextView date, title, share_count, author;

        public ViewHolder(@NonNull View itemView) {
            super(itemView);

            date = itemView.findViewById(R.id.tvDate);
            title = itemView.findViewById(R.id.tvTitle);
            share_count = itemView.findViewById(R.id.tvShareCount);
            author = itemView.findViewById(R.id.tvAuthor);

        }
    }
}

Note that there are insufficent codes given, such as the layout files, you need to prepare yourself. This article aims to tutor on implementing Firebase blog post system.

That’s all, hope you guys enjoy this article.

next_post.title: 解決 PopupMenu 複選框點選後自動重置為未勾選狀態
next_post.text: 今天要寫 PopupMenu 的時候，發現在要用點選複選框的項目會重置狀態，一般我都會這樣定義一個 PopupMenu 的。 當按鈕 點擊後便執行： 但這樣做，如果你不是要做被選框，只是純粹一個項目的話，這樣問題不大，不然會有一個問題，就是複選框勾選後會自動重置到未勾選狀態。 ...
